authoritative_sources.title: Menu Labeling Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the primary U.S. federal authority for restaurant menu-labeling requirements. This page identifies covered establishments, calorie-disclosure requirements, and written nutrition-information expectations for standard menu items.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: menu, calorie, and disclosure prerequisite validation

authoritative_sources.title: A Labeling Guide for Restaurants and Retail Establishments Selling Away-From-Home Foods - Part II (Menu Labeling Requirements in Accordance with 21 CFR 101.11)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's menu-labeling guidance explains standard menu item handling, disclosure formats, temporary menu-item treatment, written nutrition records, and FDA's cooperative enforcement posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: menu-item setup and evidence sufficiency review

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Food Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes the Food Code as the public model used by regulators to govern restaurants and retail food establishments and describes it as the scientifically sound technical and legal basis for regulating the retail and food service segment.

authoritative_sources.title: Addition to the 2022 Food Code - Sesame added as a Major Food Allergen
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's retail-food factsheet gives current operational guidance for allergen notification and labeling impacts inside food service establishments and directly ties restaurant menu handling to allergen disclosure risk.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guest-impact safety escalation and allergen notice review

authoritative_sources.title: FTC, Illinois Attorney General Take Action Against Grubhub for Harming Diners, Workers, and Small Businesses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for unfair or deceptive pricing and customer-treatment practices. This December 31, 2024 action is directly relevant to restaurant delivery fee disclosures, blocked guest funds, cancellation handling, and evidence-backed claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: consumer-pricing, refund, and blocked-funds boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Security Standards Council Official Site
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized industry standards body for payment-card security and states that it develops and drives adoption of data security standards and resources for safe payments worldwide.

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm brand, location, franchise or corporate status, menu scope, approved ticket, guest-impact context, and channel list before accepting work and determine whether the request is in scope or blocked.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Menu, price, and disclosure prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify menu-item master data, PLUs, combos, modifiers, calorie or allergen disclosure support, approved pricing records, fee-display requirements, and location rollout timing before execution proceeds.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Channel publication and order or payment reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Review POS, menu board, kiosk, marketplace, booking, and order records to determine whether a menu or price change is live everywhere it must be and whether guest-facing totals match the authoritative record.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Refund-policy and exception queue progression
workflow_stages.description: Move guest price disputes, unavailable-item defects, comp requests, and policy exceptions into explicit blocked, rework, or escalated states with evidence and owner routing instead of silent closure.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Guest-safety, allergen, fraud, and loss-prevention boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve food-safety, allergen, payment-security, and fraud boundaries whenever customer harm, payment abuse, blocked funds, or suspicious refund patterns affect the workflow.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when proof of channel publication, test transactions, or other closure evidence exists and tenant, franchise, and jurisdictional controls stricter than the public baseline have been applied.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

artifacts: menu item masters, PLUs, combo definitions, modifier groups, recipe references, and ingredient or allergen declarations
approved price-change tickets, promotion calendars, menu board assets, channel publication logs, and store rollout plans
POS checks, receipts, order tickets, booking or reservation records, order-management events, and guest account histories
refund logs, comp and void records, service-recovery notes, chargeback notices, blocked-funds complaints, and marketplace support threads
inventory states, 86 lists, product availability notes, franchise exception requests, and escalation packages
written nutrition information, calorie support records, and location or channel scope approvals
systems_of_record: digital menu board or kiosk publishing platform
delivery marketplace portals or order integrations
service desk or support ticket systems
payment processor or payment support records
PMS when restaurant operations are hotel- or hospitality-linked

allowed: Classify menu and pricing operations requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete menu and price execution work based on documented publication state and evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed execution summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required menu, price, guest, order, payment, refund, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing approval, refund or comp override authority, legal interpretation, guest-safety ownership, or fraud and loss-prevention adjudication.
A franchise, marketplace, PMS, or jurisdictional policy materially conflicts with the public baseline.
Customer harm, allergen exposure, blocked funds, chargeback, or suspicious refund activity changes whether the lane may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of completion.
Override pricing, refund, fee-disclosure, or safety policy without documented authority.
Adjudicate fraud, chargebacks, guest injury, or allergen incidents inside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: POS, marketplace, and menu-board prices diverge and the lane closes the work anyway
a guest-facing refund or comp exception is granted without the required policy authority
allergen or disclosure defects are treated as routine menu cleanup rather than customer-impact escalation
blocked guest funds or delivery-fee issues are over-resolved without evidence or escalation
high-volume price rollouts hide store-level failures because exception handling is skipped
payment-security or fraud references are treated as permission to adjudicate disputes
restaurant menu and pricing work drifts into pricing strategy, guest-services adjudication, or incident management ownership

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

workflow_questions_answered.question: What booking, order, payment, or guest records govern the lane?
workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane depends on POS checks and receipts, order tickets, booking or reservation records when relevant, guest profiles, refund and comp logs, marketplace order events, payment support records, and the approved menu or price-change ticket.

workflow_questions_answered.question: What refund, pricing, or policy constraints limit the workflow?
workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane can execute only already-approved menu and price changes and can package but not approve policy exceptions. Refunds, comps, blocked-funds issues, price overrides, and fee-disclosure exceptions depend on tenant policy, franchise rules, and jurisdiction-specific obligations.

workflow_questions_answered.question: Where does the lane stop and pricing, safety, or exception authority begin?
workflow_questions_answered.answer: The lane stops when pricing strategy, refund exception approval, legal interpretation, guest-safety ownership, allergen-incident handling, or fraud and loss-prevention adjudication would determine the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local fee-disclosure, refund, and service-charge requirements vary by jurisdiction
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package records this as a tenant-supplied runtime dependency and refuses confident synthesis when jurisdiction changes the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Federal menu-labeling coverage does not apply uniformly to all restaurant formats
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Coverage depends on chain status and establishment facts, so the lane requires tenant grounding before treating 21 CFR 101.11-style controls as mandatory.
